note: Committed baseline — deliberately updated by explicit commit, never auto-pinned. See frontend/EVAL.md for methodology and harness.test.ts for how this file is consumed. Floor values are lower bounds the harness asserts >= against on every CI run. 2026-04-24: two fixtures swapped (fr-employment→fr-commercial-lease, de-saas-dpa→de-employment), every intra-doc golden question touching those fixtures was re-authored by claude-opus-4-7 and hand-reviewed by SG in the same sweep, and the latent `c.text` bug in golden-rerank-scores.freeze.test.ts was fixed along the way (every prior hybrid_rerank freeze shipped an all-zero cache — see the hybrid_rerank row note for details).

hybrid_metadata.note: Identical to `hybrid` on the current golden set: statute aliases in `query-analysis.ts` fire on ~0 of 48 questions (golden set uses natural-language phrasing, not legally-literate citations). Category boost was tried at 0.006 and regressed overall recall@1 by 0.062 (uniform boost across all same-category clauses adds noise without signal); disabled. Infrastructure retained for Arc 3 cross-contract search where metadata is the primary discriminator. See EVAL.md §6 for the full reasoning.

hybrid_graph.note: Identical to `hybrid_metadata` on the current golden set. Widening is strictly additive (tail-append of cross-ref neighbours + definitions below the existing fused+boosted rank), so it can only lift a gold clause that was outside top-20 on BM25+cosine — none of the 48 golden questions have that profile. An earlier multiplicative-boost variant (1.5× on widening ids) regressed overall recall@1 by 0.083 on definition-heavy fixtures; disabled. The additive layer stays on for production chat context — graph widening's job is context completeness, not relevance re-ranking. See EVAL.md §7 for the full reasoning.

hybrid_rerank.note: First baseline after fixing the `c.text`→`c.clause_text` bug in golden-rerank-scores.freeze.test.ts — every prior hybrid_rerank freeze shipped all-zero rerank scores, which made the hybrid_rerank layer indistinguishable from hybrid_graph (the cross-encoder was effectively a no-op). Post-fix, Jina `jina-reranker-v2-base-multilingual` produces real lift over hybrid_graph: overall R@1 0.854 → 0.896 (+0.042), MRR 0.898 → 0.936 (+0.038). The lift is concentrated on de-employment (R@1 0.375 → 0.875) and medium/hard tier questions where the cross-encoder's ability to read full clause text disambiguates semantically close candidates. nl-freelance R@1 regresses 0.875 → 0.625 — Jina prefers a near-miss on three of the eight NL easy questions — but the overall + MRR gain more than offsets, and recall@5 is preserved at 0.979. See EVAL.md §8 for the full reasoning.

cross_doc.note: Cross-contract retrieval — 24-question golden set (`cross-doc-questions.ts`), merged candidate pool (83 clauses across 6 fixtures — nl-freelance 13, fr-commercial-lease 15, de-employment 16, es-saas-services 12, it-employment 13, pl-distribution 14), pure pgvector-style cosine ranking (no BM25 — mirrors `services/semantic_search.py`). 2026-04-24: 10 of 24 entries were re-targeted or re-authored by claude-opus-4-7 after the fr-commercial-lease + de-employment fixture swap destroyed their old clause_index anchors, and the re-authored entries were hand-reviewed by SG in the same sweep. Post-swap numbers are a modest lift over the previous floor (easy R@1 0.875 → 1.000, overall R@1 0.833 → 0.875) driven by the re-authored questions leaning on unique lexical anchors (overtime flat-rate, restaurant lease).
